questions.stem: What is the primary purpose of Cost Management in project management?
choices: To ensure the project finishes early
To manage project costs within the approved budget
questions.explanation: Cost management focuses on planning, estimating, budgeting, and controlling costs so the project stays within the approved budget.

questions.stem: Which process produces the Cost Management Plan?
questions.explanation: Plan Cost Management defines how project costs will be planned, structured, and controlled.

questions.stem: Which process produces cost estimates for project activities?
questions.explanation: Estimate Costs develops approximate costs of resources needed to complete project work.

questions.stem: Which process combines individual work package estimates to create the overall project budget?
questions.explanation: Determine Budget aggregates cost estimates to establish the cost baseline.

questions.stem: Which process monitors project spending and identifies cost variances?
questions.explanation: Control Costs measures performance and compares actual costs to the cost baseline.

questions.stem: What is the main output of Determine Budget?
questions.explanation: The cost baseline is the approved time phased project budget used to measure cost performance.

questions.stem: Why are cost baselines important?
questions.explanation: The cost baseline allows the project manager to compare actual costs with planned spending.

questions.stem: Which process uses Earned Value Management to measure performance?
questions.explanation: Earned Value Management is a key tool used in Control Costs.

questions.stem: Which project management knowledge area focuses on planning, estimating, budgeting, and controlling project costs so the project can be completed within the approved budget?
questions.explanation: Cost Management includes the processes required to plan, estimate, budget, finance, fund, manage, and control project costs.

questions.stem: A project manager creates a document that explains how costs will be estimated, monitored, and controlled throughout the project. What document is being created?
questions.explanation: The Cost Management Plan defines how costs will be estimated, tracked, and controlled during the project.

questions.stem: A project manager is calculating the expected cost for each work package in the Work Breakdown Structure. Which process is being performed?
questions.explanation: Estimate Costs is the process of developing an approximation of the cost of resources needed to complete project activities.

questions.stem: A project manager combines the estimated costs of all work packages and establishes the total approved project budget. Which process is this?
questions.explanation: Determine Budget aggregates individual estimates to establish the cost baseline for the project.

questions.stem: During the project the manager compares planned spending with actual spending and investigates differences. Which process is being performed?
questions.explanation: Control Costs monitors project spending and identifies cost variances.

questions.stem: Which document represents the approved time phased project budget used to measure cost performance?
questions.explanation: The cost baseline is the approved budget distributed over time and used to measure performance.

questions.stem: Which of the following best describes 'Budget at Completion' (BAC)?
choices: The total approved budget for the project
questions.explanation: BAC represents the total planned cost for the entire project.

questions.stem: A project manager wants to monitor cost performance by comparing actual cost with the planned budget. What document is required to perform this comparison?
questions.explanation: The cost baseline provides the planned spending that actual spending can be compared against.

questions.stem: A project manager adds extra funds to account for known risks identified during planning. What are these funds called?
questions.explanation: Reserves are included in the budget to account for risks and uncertainty.

questions.stem: A project manager wants to understand how efficiently the project is using its budget compared to the work completed. Which technique should be used?
questions.explanation: Earned Value Management integrates scope, schedule, and cost to measure project performance.

questions.stem: Which Earned Value term represents the planned cost of work scheduled to be completed?
questions.explanation: Planned Value is the budgeted cost of the work that was scheduled to be completed by a specific time.

questions.stem: Which Earned Value term represents the budgeted value of work that has actually been completed?
questions.explanation: Earned Value represents how much of the planned work has actually been completed.

questions.stem: Which Earned Value term represents the real money spent performing work?
questions.explanation: Actual Cost represents the actual money spent performing project work.

questions.stem: A project manager wants to know if the project is currently over budget or under budget. Which metric should be calculated?
questions.explanation: Cost Variance compares Earned Value with Actual Cost to determine cost performance.

questions.stem: Which metric compares completed work with planned work to determine if the project is ahead or behind schedule?
questions.explanation: Schedule Variance measures the difference between Earned Value and Planned Value.

questions.stem: A project manager wants to forecast the total cost of the project based on current performance. Which metric is used?
questions.explanation: Estimate at Completion forecasts the expected final cost of the project.

questions.stem: Which metric represents how much additional money is required to finish the project?
questions.explanation: Estimate to Complete represents the expected cost needed to finish the remaining work.

questions.stem: Which performance index measures cost efficiency?
questions.explanation: Cost Performance Index measures cost efficiency by comparing earned value to actual cost.

questions.stem: Which performance index measures schedule efficiency?
questions.explanation: Schedule Performance Index measures how efficiently the project schedule is being used.

questions.stem: Why is Earned Value Management valuable to project managers?
choices: It integrates cost, schedule, and scope performance
questions.explanation: Earned Value Management integrates scope, schedule, and cost to evaluate project performance.
